subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Newton's Universal Gravitation
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Newton's Universal Gravitation.

hook.text: Isaac Newton unified the heavens and the Earth. The same force that drops an apple curves the Moon's orbit.

buildup.text: His law of universal gravitation says every mass attracts every other mass. The strength fades with distance squared.

discovery.text: Newton proved that Kepler's laws are consequences of gravity. One equation explained all planetary motion.

twist.text: Newton invented calculus to solve these problems. He published it in 1687—and then feuded with Leibniz over credit.

climax.text: His work dominated physics for over 200 years—until Einstein showed gravity is actually curved spacetime.

punchline.text: One force, one law. Apples and moons obey the same equation.

quiz.question: What did Newton's law of universal gravitation unify?
options: The force governing objects on Earth and the motion of celestial bodies
The orbits of comets and the behavior of light
